properties.name: Hetauda-Dhalkebar-Inaruwa 400 kV backbone
properties.geometry_basis: Manual corridor trace derived from the World Bank HDDTL RAP route description, affected municipality/VDC corridor, and route-location figure, with source-named place anchors geocoded from OSM/Nominatim where local grid anchors were unavailable.
properties.notes: Replaces the oversimplified RPGCL overview-vector selection for the public connected layer. This is a document-grounded corridor trace, not a tower-by-tower alignment. The RAP states the first 18.6 km is hill section, the next 23.4 km is hill-to-Terai, and the remaining route follows the Terai corridor. NEA FY2024/25 still lists Hetauda-Dhalkebar under construction.

properties.name: Hetauda-Dhalkebar-Inaruwa 400 kV backbone
properties.geometry_basis: Manual corridor trace derived from the World Bank HDDTL RAP route description, affected municipality/VDC corridor, and route-location figure, with source-named place anchors geocoded from OSM/Nominatim where local grid anchors were unavailable.
properties.notes: Replaces the oversimplified RPGCL overview-vector selection for the public connected layer. This is a document-grounded corridor trace, not a tower-by-tower alignment. The RAP terminates the line at the new Duhabi substation at Hanif Tole/Bhokraha; the public map labels the modern terminal as Inaruwa 400 kV substation.

properties.name: MCA Central 400 kV: Lapsiphedi-Ratmate
properties.geometry_basis: Manual route-level trace from MCA Annex D-1 baseline alignment atlas, cross-checked against MCC Compact segment topology and RPGCL map labels.
properties.notes: One of five MCA Central 400 kV project segments. Sheet range is assigned from atlas page order and official segment proportions; contact-sheet review outputs retain page-level audit context.

properties.name: MCA Central 400 kV: Ratmate-New Hetauda
properties.geometry_basis: Manual route-level trace from MCA Annex D-1 baseline alignment atlas, cross-checked against MCC Compact segment topology and RPGCL map labels.
properties.notes: This segment branches south from Ratmate to New Hetauda. It shares the exact Ratmate coordinate with the Lapsiphedi and New Damauli segments to preserve topology.

properties.name: MCA Central 400 kV: Ratmate-New Damauli
properties.geometry_basis: Manual route-level trace from MCA Annex D-1 baseline alignment atlas, cross-checked against MCC Compact segment topology and RPGCL map labels.
properties.notes: This segment is the westward Ratmate branch toward New Damauli. It shares the exact Ratmate and New Damauli coordinates with adjacent MCA segments.

properties.name: MCA Central 400 kV: New Damauli-New Butwal
properties.geometry_basis: Manual route-level trace from MCA Annex D-1 baseline alignment atlas, cross-checked against MCC Compact segment topology and RPGCL map labels.
properties.notes: This segment continues the western MCA corridor from New Damauli to New Butwal. It shares exact endpoint coordinates with the adjacent New Damauli and border-link segments.

properties.name: MCA Central 400 kV: New Butwal-India border
properties.geometry_basis: Manual route-level trace from MCA Annex D-1 baseline alignment atlas, cross-checked against MCC Compact segment topology, RPGCL map labels, and the processed Gorakhpur-New Butwal border gateway.
properties.notes: Nepal-side border link only. This segment ends at the processed Belahiya/New Butwal border-corridor gateway and does not guess the Indian-side route.

properties.name: Marsyangdi Corridor Udipur-Markichowk-Bharatpur 220 kV
properties.source_page_or_sheet: NEA Marsyangdi RAP pages 22-24; Figure 2.1 and project salient features
properties.geometry_basis: Manual route-level trace from the RAP location map and text. The RAP gives 64.45 route-km in text and labels the Udipur-Markichowk-Bharatpur line as 67 km on Figure 2.1.
properties.notes: Schematic corridor trace for the Udipur-Markichowk-Bharatpur/Aptari line, not tower-level alignment. Khudi-Udipur should remain a separate scope item unless a route-grade source confirms it belongs in this segment.

properties.name: Hetauda-Bharatpur-Bardaghat 220 kV corridor
properties.source_page_or_sheet: RPGCL 2021 geospatial PDF, existing/under-construction 220 kV layer
properties.geometry_basis: Manual selection of the Hetauda-New Bharatpur 220 kV official-vector trace from the RPGCL geospatial transmission map, cross-checked against NEA reporting that states the Hetauda-Bharatpur section is about 73 km.
properties.notes: This feature replaces over-selected public RPGCL fragments for the HBB corridor. It is source-vector geometry selected at corridor level, not tower-level survey.

properties.name: Hetauda-Bharatpur-Bardaghat 220 kV corridor
properties.source_page_or_sheet: NEA Bharatpur-Bardaghat SIA chapter 1 and 3; route length and affected VDC corridor
properties.geometry_basis: Manual corridor-level trace from New Bharatpur/Aaptari to Bardaghat using NEA SIA route length and affected VDC corridor: Bharatpur, Gaindakot/Mukundapur, Devchuli/Rajahar, Kawasoti, Dumkibas/Binayi-Tribeni, and Bardaghat.
properties.notes: This is a source-bounded corridor trace, not an engineering alignment. It replaces over-selected RPGCL fragments while preserving the raw RPGCL linework for audit.

properties.name: Dana-Kushma-New Butwal 220 kV corridor
properties.source_page_or_sheet: RPGCL 2021 geospatial PDF existing/under-construction 220 kV layer; NEA Transmission Annual Book 2077 pp. 144-145 route-length control
properties.geometry_basis: Manual selection of the official-vector Dana-Kushma 220 kV trace from the RPGCL geospatial transmission map, cross-checked against NEA FY2019/20 route length and FY2024/25 existing-line inventory.
properties.notes: NEA FY2019/20 states 39.57 route-km for Dana-Kushma; NEA FY2024/25 lists 79.6 circuit-km, consistent with double-circuit accounting.

properties.name: Dana-Kushma-New Butwal 220 kV corridor
properties.source_page_or_sheet: RPGCL 2021 geospatial PDF existing/under-construction 220 kV layer; NEA Transmission Annual Book 2077 pp. 144-145 and NEA FY2024/25 p. 59 status text
properties.geometry_basis: Manual selection of the official-vector Kushma-New Butwal 220 kV trace from the RPGCL geospatial transmission map, cross-checked against NEA FY2019/20 route length and FY2024/25 commissioning/status text.
properties.notes: NEA FY2019/20 states 88 route-km for Kushma-New Butwal; NEA FY2024/25 lists 176 circuit-km and says New Butwal became fully operational after this line was finished.

properties.name: Western Nepal 132 kV backbone
properties.source_page_or_sheet: RPGCL 2021 geospatial PDF existing 132 kV layer; NEA FY2024/25 pp. 162-163 existing high-voltage line inventory
properties.geometry_basis: Reconstructed route from official RPGCL 2021 geospatial vector strokes, controlled against NEA FY2024/25 operational 132 kV inventory names and circuit-km lengths.
properties.notes: NEA FY2024/25 existing-line inventory lists Butwal-Shivapur-Lamahi-Kohalpur as a 430 circuit-km double-circuit 132 kV line, equivalent to about 215 route-km.

properties.name: Western Nepal 132 kV backbone
properties.source_page_or_sheet: RPGCL 2021 geospatial PDF existing 132 kV layer; NEA FY2024/25 pp. 162-163 existing high-voltage line inventory
properties.geometry_basis: Reconstructed route from official RPGCL 2021 geospatial vector strokes, controlled against NEA FY2024/25 operational 132 kV inventory names and circuit-km lengths.
properties.notes: NEA FY2024/25 existing-line inventory lists Kohalpur-Bhurigaun-Lumki as a 176.66 circuit-km double-circuit 132 kV line, equivalent to about 88.33 route-km.

properties.name: Western Nepal 132 kV backbone
properties.source_page_or_sheet: RPGCL 2021 geospatial PDF existing 132 kV layer; NEA FY2024/25 pp. 162-163 existing high-voltage line inventory
properties.geometry_basis: Reconstructed route from official RPGCL 2021 geospatial vector strokes, controlled against NEA FY2024/25 operational 132 kV inventory names and circuit-km lengths.
properties.notes: NEA FY2024/25 existing-line inventory lists Lamki-Pahalwanpur-Attariya-Mahendranagar (Lalpur) as a 203.12 circuit-km double-circuit 132 kV line, equivalent to about 101.56 route-km.

properties.name: Chameliya-Syaule-Attariya 132 kV line
properties.source_page_or_sheet: RPGCL 2021 geospatial PDF existing 132 kV layer; NEA FY2024/25 pp. 162-163 existing high-voltage line inventory
properties.geometry_basis: Reconstructed route from official RPGCL 2021 geospatial vector strokes, controlled against NEA FY2024/25 operational 132 kV inventory names and circuit-km lengths.
properties.notes: NEA FY2024/25 existing-line inventory lists Chameliya-Syaule-Attariya as a 262 circuit-km double-circuit 132 kV line, equivalent to about 131 route-km.

properties.name: Kohalpur-Surkhet-Dailekh 132 kV grid reach
properties.source_page_or_sheet: NEA FY2024/25 p. 35 project status narrative and p. 175 under-construction line table; RPGCL 2021 place labels
properties.geometry_basis: Manual route trace through RPGCL-labeled Kohalpur, Surkhet, and Dailekh anchors, controlled by NEA FY2024/25 project narrative: 52 km Kohalpur-Surkhet plus 32 km Surkhet-Dailekh.
properties.notes: Shown as under construction, not operational. NEA FY2024/25 reports advanced Kohalpur-Surkhet tower/foundation progress and partial Surkhet-Dailekh foundation progress.

properties.name: Kabeli / Godak-Soyak-Amarpur 132 kV corridor
properties.geometry_basis: Manual corridor trace derived from the recovered Kabeli IEE district/VDC map and route description, cross-checked against the Kabeli single-line schematic and supporting NEA RAP/SMEF packets.
properties.notes: This manual trace follows the documented bifurcation from the southern trunk up to Soyak, then east to Godak and northwest toward Phidim/Amarpur. It is materially better than the old substation spine, but should still be read as corridor geometry rather than parcel-level alignment.

properties.name: Kabeli / Godak-Soyak-Amarpur 132 kV corridor
properties.geometry_basis: Manual corridor trace derived from the recovered Kabeli IEE district/VDC map and route description, cross-checked against the Kabeli single-line schematic and supporting NEA RAP/SMEF packets.
properties.notes: This manual trace follows the documented bifurcation from the southern trunk up to Soyak, then east to Godak and northwest toward Phidim/Amarpur. It is materially better than the old substation spine, but should still be read as corridor geometry rather than parcel-level alignment.

properties.name: Kabeli / Godak-Soyak-Amarpur 132 kV corridor
properties.geometry_basis: Manual corridor trace derived from the recovered Kabeli IEE district/VDC map and route description, cross-checked against the Kabeli single-line schematic and supporting NEA RAP/SMEF packets.
properties.notes: This manual trace follows the documented bifurcation from the southern trunk up to Soyak, then east to Godak and northwest toward Phidim/Amarpur. It is materially better than the old substation spine, but should still be read as corridor geometry rather than parcel-level alignment.

properties.name: Marsyangdi Corridor Manang-Khudi-Udipur 220 kV
properties.geometry_basis: Manual corridor trace derived from the recovered upper Marsyangdi RAP location, project-area, and accessibility maps, anchored to mapped Dharapani, Khudi, Besishahar, and Udipur nodes.
properties.notes: The route follows the upper Marsyangdi valley south from Dharapani to Khudi and then bends southeast toward Udipur. This is a document-grounded corridor trace, not a tower-by-tower alignment.

properties.name: Solu Corridor Tingla-Mirchaiya 132 kV
properties.geometry_basis: Manual corridor trace derived from NEA Transmission Annual Book 2077 narrative pages on the Tingla substation and Solu Corridor transmission line, anchored to the source-stated Mirchaiya and Tingla terminals plus the Maruwa/Katari reroute location and the municipal corridor through Okhaldhunga.
properties.notes: This is a corridor-level reconstruction, not an alignment-sheet trace. The FY 2019/20 yearbook describes the line as a 90 km double-circuit route from Mirchaiya to Tingla and notes a rerouting dispute at Maruwa, Katari municipality. The FY 2024/25 inventory later lists 180 km, which likely reflects double-circuit conductor counting rather than a different path.
